title: Conscious Gaze: Adaptive Attention Mechanisms for Hallucination Mitigation in Vision-Language Models
abstract: This paper introduces Conscious Gaze (CG-VLM), a method that employs a Cognitive Demand Sensor (CDS) to monitor Harsanyi interaction variance and a Focused Consensus Induction (FCI) module to redirect attention heads back toward visual tokens, thereby mitigating hallucinations in vision-language models.

components.name: Cognitive Demand Sensor (CDS)
components.function: Monitors Harsanyi interaction variance to detect vision-text conflicts

components.name: Focused Consensus Induction (FCI)
components.function: Mechanistically redirects middle-layer attention heads back toward visual tokens

hallucination_signatures: Late Drift: 67% of hallucinations occur after visual attention drops below 20%
Function Word Amplification: Function words deepen this drift in 73% of cases
Irreversibility: Once attention shifts to text priors, probability of recovering visual grounding drops by 84%

contributions: Mechanistic insight into text inertia as a dominant cause of hallucination on POPE/CHAIR
Interaction-driven internal control using Harsanyi interaction variance as a real-time control signal

results.performance: GPT-4o rated CG-VLM highest for both fluency (9/10) and accuracy (9/10)
